signals.title: Stephens County receives Crocus solar tax-abatement application
signals.summary: Crocus Solar submitted a Stephens County tax-abatement application for a proposed 100 MW photovoltaic facility with a co-located 100 MWh battery energy storage component.
signals.quote:
APPLICATION FOR TAX ABATEMENT

 Stephens County, Texas

 This application should be filed with the County Judge's Office of Stephens County.

 1. Property Owner: Crocus Solar, LLC
 2. Texas Taxpayer ID: 32096527695
 3. NAICS: 221114
 4. Mailing Address: 585 N. Dairy Ashford Rd, Suite 760 Houston, TX 77079
 5. Telephone Number: (713) 227-7103
 6. Owner's Representative: Hyohwan Lee, Authorized Signatory
 7. Mailing Address: 585 N. Dairy Ashford Rd, Suite 760 Houston, TX 77079
 8. Telephone Number: (346) 764-7525
 9. Property Address: Please reference Exhibit A
 10. Stephens CAD PID: Please reference Exhibit A
 11. Located within City: N/A
 12. Located within ISD: Breckenridge ISD
 13. Projected Start of Construction: Q4 2026
 14. Projected Commercial Operations: Q2 2028
 15. Company Consultant: Gerald Byrd, Gerald.Byrd@ey.com, (936) 827-2900
 Troy Reed, Troy.T.Reed@ey.com, (972) 342-5997
 16. Taxing Entities: Stephens County, Breckenridge ISD, Stephens County Hospital District

Description of project: Crocus Solar, LLC anticipates constructing a 100-megawatt (MW) utility scale single axis
tracker photovoltaic facility designed to use solar power to generate electricity, as well as a co-located Battery Energy
Storage System component with an estimated storage capacity of 100-megawatt hours (50 MW 2 Hour batteries).

signals.quote:
The Commissioners Court of Mitchell County cordially invites the Public to attend a public
hearing on the topic of approval of a tax abatement agreements. At the hearing, the public is invited
to speak and present evidence for or against the tax abatement agreements. This public hearing
will be held at the following locations and times:

 December 15th, 2025
 9:00 a.m. Commissioners Courtroom
 Mitchell County Courthouse
 349 Oak St.
 Colorado City, Texas 79512

Owner of the property:

Cipher Barber Lake, LLC
1 Vanderbilt Avenue. Floor 54. New York, NY 10017

A general description of the nature of the improvements in the agreements are as follows:

The required assets for construction and operation of a data center facility, and power
cooling equipment, networking, racks, hardware, software, utility, infrastructure, security
systems, logistics improvements, related buildings, and any and all other associated forms of
real property improvements and tangible personal property. All improvements and personal
property at the data center are intended to be subject to the abatement.

The estimated cost of improvements is as follows:

 -Cipher Barber Lake, LLC

The Reinvestment Zone subject to these agreements is the Cipher Reinvestment Zone. It includes

signals.summary: Floyd County gives notice of possible action on a Chapter 312 tax-abatement agreement for South Plains Wind Energy’s planned replacement of 100 turbine nacelles and rotors.
signals.quote:
PURPOSE: The Floyd County Commissioners Court will discuss and take possible action regarding
a tax abatement agreement with South Plains Wind Energy, LLC, pursuant to Chapter 312 of the

Texas Tax Code.

Property Owner and Applicant (“Applicant”) for Tax Abatement: South Plains Wind Energy,
LLC

Reinvestment Zone: South Plains Wind Energy Reinvestment Zone

Property in Reinvestment Zone: See attached map and description provided in Exhibit “A”
attached hereto and incorporated herein for all purposes.

signals.title: Harley Hand Solar tax-abatement amendment approved
signals.summary: Nolan County approved an amendment to the Harley Hand Solar tax-abatement arrangement.
signals.quote:
5. CONSIDER AND POSSIBLE ACTION ON AMENDING THE HARLEY HAND SOLAR AND STORAGE
PROJECT TAX ABATEMENT AGREEMENT PURSUANT TO CHAPTER 312 OF THE TEXAS TAX CODE
BETWEEN NOLAN COUNTY (THE ‘COUNTY’) AND HECATE HARLEY HAND SOLAR, LLC AND HEATE
HARLEY HAND STORAGE, LLC (COLLECTIVELY THE ‘HECATE’). IN COMLIANCE WITH SECTION
312.207(c) OF THE TEXAS TAX CODE IN PERTINENT PART, THE PROPOSED TAX ABATEMENT
AGREEMENTS.

A. HECATE HARLEY HAND SOLAR, LLC AND HECATE HARLEY HAND STORAGE, LLC, OWNERS OF
INTERESTS IN LANDS ELIGIBLE FOR ABATEMENT AGREEMENTS WITHIN THE REINVESTMENT ZONE
SUBMITTED APPLICATIONS FOR TAX ABATEMENT AGREEMENTS.

B. THE ABATEMENT AGREEMENT IS PROPOSED WITHIN THE HARLEY HAND SOLAR AND STORAGE
PROJECT REINVESTMENT ZONE DESIGNATED BY THE COMMISSIONERS COURT OF NOLAN COUNTY,
TEXAS ON JANUARY 22, 2024 LOCATED IN NORTHEASTERN NOLAN COUNTY. A MAP OF THE EXISTENT
REINVESTMENT ZONE IS AVAILABLE FOR INSPECTION IN THE OFFICE OF THE COUNTY JUDGE.

Cc. THE PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS ARE RELATED TO THE CONSTRUCTION OF SOLAR ENERGY AND
STORAGE FACILITY INCLUDING SOLAR PANELS, TRACKING SYSTEMS, BATTERY ENERGY STORAGE
SYSTEMS (AS SUCH TERM IS USED IN COMMON LANGUAGE), INVERTERS, TRANSORMERS,
FOUNDATIONS, CONTAINERS, CABLING, COLLECTION LINES, COLLECTION STATIONS,
TRANSMISSION LINES, SUBSTATIONS, INTERCONNECTION FACILITIES, OPERATION ANDF
MAINTENANCE FACILITIES, AND OTHER INFRASTRUCTURE, MATERIALS, AND EQUIPMENT
CUSTOMARY AND NECESSARY FOR THE OPERATION OF A SOLAR POWER ELECTRIC GENERATION
AND BATTERY ENERGY STORAGE FACILITY.

D. THE ESTIMATED COST OF THE IMPROVEMENTTS SHALL BE IN EXCESS OF $300,000,000.00 WITH RESPECT
TO THE SOLAR PROJECT AND $70,000,000.00 WITH RESPECT TO THE STORAGE PROJECT.

Nolan County Commissioners’ Court Minutes ~ Page 1

Motion was made by Commissioner Mahaffey, seconded by Commissioner Willman to approve amending the
Harley Hand Solar and Storage Project Tax Abatement. Vote was taken and members of the court unanimously
voted yes.

signals.title: Runnels solar tax-abatement eligibility reauthorized
signals.summary: The county adopted a resolution reauthorizing tax-abatement guidelines that include photovoltaic solar facilities.
signals.quote:
Renewable Energy Facility” means buildings and structures, including but not limited
to electricity generating equipment (such as wind turbines or photovoltaic solar panels),
electric transmission lines, electric power substations, electrical gathering equipment,
communications systems and roads, fixed machinery and equipment, used or to be used
to provide electrical energy, and which meet the definition of “Renewable Energy
Electric Generation” in Chapter 313 of the Texas Tax Code.

(s)

STATE OF TEXAS COMMISSIONERS COURT
COUNTY OF RUNNELS RESOLUTION NO.

RESOLUTION OF THE COURT

A RESOLUTION OF THE COMMISSIONERS COURT OF RUNNELS COUNTY, TEXAS
ELECTING TO PARTICIPATE IN TAX ABATEMENT AGREEMENTS, AND REAUTHORIZING
GUIDELINES AND CRITERIA GOVERNING TAX ABATEMENT FOR ECONOMIC
DEVELOPMENT PROSPECTS IN RUNNELS COUNTY

RESOLUTION WHEREAS, § 312.002, Texas Tax Code, requires a taxing unit to adopt a
resolution establishing guidelines and criteria governing tax abatement agreements and stating
that the taxing unit elects to become eligible to participate in tax abatement; and

WHEREAS, the Commissioners Court of Runnels has expressed its intent to consider tax
abatements and adopted Tax Abatement Guidelines and Criteria; and

WHEREAS, pursuant to § 312.002, the Tax Abatement Guidelines and Criteria are effective for
two years unless amended or repealed by a vote of the Commissioners Court; and

WHEREAS, the Runnels County Commissioners Court desires to reauthorize its Tax Abatement
Guidelines and Criteria; and,

WHEREAS, the Runnels County Commissioners Court desires to be eligible to participate in tax
abatements.

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE COMMISSIONERS COURT OF RUNNELS
COUNTY, TEXAS:

PART 1: The County of Runnels elects to participate in tax abatement agreements pursuant to
Chapter 312 of the Texas Tax Code, and that the Guidelines and Criteria attached as Exhibit “A
signals.authorityCaveat: The guidelines preserve county discretion and do not approve or restrict a specific project.

signals.title: Runnels guidelines include energy-storage facilities
signals.summary: The adopted guidelines list energy-storage facilities as eligible for discretionary reinvestment-zone and abatement consideration.
signals.quote:
Runnels County, hereinafter referred to as “County” is committed to the promotion of quality
development in all parts of the County and to improving the quality of life for its citizens. In order to help
meet these goals, the County will consider recommending tax incentives, which may include the
designation of reinvestment zones, accepting applications for tax abatement, and entering into tax
abatement agreements to stimulate growth and development.

It is the intent of the County that such incentives will be provided in accordance with the
procedures and criteria in this document and in Chapter 312 of the Texas Tax Code. However, nothing in
these Guidelines and Criteria shall imply or suggest to be construed to imply or suggest that tax entities
are under any obligation to provide any incentives to any applicant. All such applicants for tax incentives
shall be considered on an individual basis for both the qualification for tax abatement and the amount of
any tax abatement. The adoption of these Guidelines and Criteria shall not create any property, contract,
or other legal right in any person to have the governing body consider or grant a specific application or

request for tax abatement.

Only that increase in the fair market value of the property directly resultant from the
development, redevelopment, and improvement specified in the contract will be eligible for abatement.
All abatement contracts will be for a term no longer than allowed by law. Additionally, the Runnels
County Commissioners Court reserves the right to negotiate a tax abatement agreement in order to
compete favorably with other communities.

I. DEFINITIONS

The attached Glossary is a list of words with their definitions that are found in this document, and
the Glossary is incorporated herein by reference.

tM. GUIDELINES AND CRITERIA

In order to be eligible for designation as a reinvestment zone and to receive tax abatement, the
planned improvement as a minimum must meet the following:
(a) Be an authorized Facility. A Facility may be eligible for abatement if it is a(n):
Aquaculture/Agriculture Facility
Distribution Center Facility
Manufacturing Facility
Office Building
Regional Entertainment/Tourism Facility

Research Facility

Regional Service Facility

Historic Building in designated area
Renewable Energy Facility

Energy Storage Facility
signals.authorityCaveat: Eligibility creates no right to an incentive and is not a BESS restriction.
signals.model: ChatGPT Pro extended review + deterministic local verification

supportingSources.title: November 13, 2025 Public Hearing
supportingSources.quote:
Pursuant to Texas Tax Code Chapter 312, the Concho County Commissioners Court
hereby gives notice that it will conduct a public hearing at the regularly scheduled
meeting of the Court to be held on (Thursday, November 13)/2025 at 145 pam) in the
Concho County Courthouse, 152 N. Roberts Street, Paint Rock, Texas, concerning the
approval of a restated tax abatement agreement with Concho Pearl Solar, LLC, applicant
and proposed owner of a combination solar energy generation/battery energy storage
facility.

The proposed agreements would each provide for a tax abatement and payments in lieu of
taxes (PILOTs) for properties located within reinvestment zones previously established
by resolution of the Concho County Commissioners Court. The reinvestment zone and
proposed improvements comprise a solar photovoltaic electric generation system and a
battery energy storage system and related equipment

signals.title: Jones City Energy Storage incentive and road agreements approved
signals.summary: The county approved the tax-abatement and road-use, maintenance, and crossing agreements for the battery-storage project.
signals.quote:
DURING THE REGULARLY SCHEDULED MEETING OF NOVEMBER 10, 2025 AT 9
O’CLOCK A.M., THE JONES COUNTY COMMISSIONERS WILL DISCUSS, CONSIDER
AND TAKE ACTION UPON A RESOLUTION AND ORDER APPROVING THE TERMS OF A

TAX ABATEMENT AGREEMENT AND A ROAD USE, MAINTENANCE AND CROSSING
AGREEMENT BETWEEN JONES COUNTY AND JONES CITY ENERGY STORAGE, LLC.
JONES CITY ENERGY STORAGE, LLC HAS FILED AN APPLICATION FOR A TAX
ABATEMENT AGREEMENT WITH JONES COUNTY FOR THE BATTERY ENERGY
SYSTEM IT PROPOSES TO CONSTRUCT WHICH WILL CONSIST OF A BATTERY
ENERGY STORAGE SYSTEM CONSISTING OF BATTERY ENCLOSURES, POWER
CONVERSION SYSTEMS AND RELATED FACILITIES WITH AN ESTIMATED CAPITAL
COST OF APPROXIMATELY $120,000,000.00.

DURING THIS MEETING, THE COMMISSIONERS WILL CONDUCT A PUBLIC HEARING
CONCERNING THE CREATION AND THE RENEWAL OF A REINVESTMENT ZONE,
WHERE THE BATTERY ENERGY SYSTEM WOULD BE LOCATED. IN DETERMINING
WHETHER TO DESIGNATE OR RENEW THE DESIGNATION OF THE REINVESTMENT
ZONE, THE COMMISSIONERS COURT WILL CONSIDER WHETHER OR NOT THE
PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS ARE FEASIBLE AND PRACTICAL AND WOULD BEA
BENEFIT TO THE LAND AND JONES COUNTY AFTER THE EXPIRATION OF AN
ABATEMENT AGREEMENT ENTERED INTO UNDER CHAPTER 312 AND WHETHER THE
DESIGNATION WILL CONTRIBUTE TO THE RETENTION OR EXPANSION OF PRIMARY
EMPLOYMENT AND WILL ATTRACT MAJOR INVESTMENT IN THE ZONE THAT WILL
BENEFIT THE ZONE AND WILL CONTRIBUTE TO THE ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT OF
THE COUNTY. UPON COMPLETION OF THE PUBLIC HEARING, THE COMMISSIONERS
WILL ALSO DISCUSS, CONSIDER AND TAKE ACTION UPON A RESOLUTION AND
ORDER RENEWING AND ALSO DESIGNATING REINVESTMENT ZONE, WHICH IF
RENEWED AND DESIGNATED, WILL BE THE “JONES CITY SOLAR REINVESTMENT

ZONE”.

ALL INTERESTED PERSONS MAY APPEAR AND PRESENT TESTIMONY REGARDING
DESIGNATION OF THE REINVESTMENT ZONE. THE COMMISSIONERS WILL LIMIT
THE TESTIMONY TO 5 MINUTES PER SPEAKER.

IF DURING THE COURSE OF THE MEETING COVERED BY THIS NOTICE THE
COMMISSIONERS SHOULD DETERMINE THAT A CLOSED OR EXECUTIVE SESSION OF
THE COMMISSIONERS SHOULD BE HELD OR IS REQUIRED IN RELATION TO AN ITEM
NOTICED IN THIS MEETING NOTICE, THEN SUCH CLOSED OR EXECUTIVE MEETING
OR SESSION AS AUTHORIZED BY THE OPEN MEETINGS ACT WILL BE HELD BY THE
COMMISSIONERS AT THE DATE, HOUR, AND PLACE GIVEN IN THIS NOTICE OR AS
SOON AFTER THE COMMENCEMENT OF THE MEETING COVERED BY THIS NOTICE
AS THE COMMISSIONERS MAY CONVENIENTLY MEET IN SUCH CLOSED OR
EXECUTIVE MEETING OR SESSION CONCERNING ANY AND ALL SUBJECTS AND FOR
ANY AND ALL PURPOSES PERMITTED BY SECTION 551.071-551.084 OF THE OPEN
MEETINGS ACT, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO: SECTION 551.071 — FOR THE
PURPOSE OF A PRIVATE CONSULTATION WITH THE COMMISSIONERS’ ATTORNEY
ON ANY OR ALL SUBJECTS OR MATTERS AUTHORIZED BY LAW.

THE COMMISSIONERS RESERVE THE RIGHT TO CHANGE THE ORDER OF ITEMS ON
THE AGENDA. AGENDA ITEMS MAY BE TAKEN OUT OF SEQUENCE TO
ACCOMMODATE PERSONS APPEARING BEFORE THE COMMISSIONERS AND/OR TO
AID IN THE EFFECTIVENESS OF THE MEETING.

SHOULD ANY FINAL ACTION, FINAL DECISION, OR FINAL VOTE BE REQUIRED IN

THE OPINION OF THE COMMISSIONERS WITH REGARD TO ANY MATTER

CONSIDERED IN SUCH CLOSED OR EXECUTIVE MEETING OR SESSION, THEN SUCH

FINAL ACTION, FINAL DECISION, OR FINAL VOTE SHALL BE AT EITHER:

A. THE OPEN MEETING COVERED BY THIS NOTICE UPON THE RECONVENING OF
THIS PUBLIC MEETING, OR

B. AT A SUBSEQUENT PUBLIC MEETING OF THE COMMISSIONERS UPON NOTICE
THEREOF, AS THE COMMISSIONERS SHALL DETERMINE.

Judge Spurgin opened for public comment at 9:07 AM. No public comment was made. Judge Spurgin
closed public comment at 9:08 AM.

Commissioner Spalding entered a motion to approve the Resolution and Order Approving the Renewal
of and the Designation of the Jones City Solar Reinvestment Zone. Commissioner Vivian seconded the
motion with Commissioner Spraberry, Commissioner Collett and Judge Spurgin voting aye.

See Exhibit G.

Commissioner Vivian entered a motion to approve the Tax Abatement Agreement between Jones County
and Jones City Energy Storage, LLC and Resolution and Order Approving a Tax Abatement Agreement
and a Road Use, Maintenance and Crossing Agreement between Jones County and Jones City Energy
Storage, LLC. Commissioner Spalding seconded the motion with Commissioner Spraberry,
Commissioner Collett and Judge Spurgin voting aye. See Exhibit H.
signals.authorityCaveat: These are project-facilitation agreements, not a restrictive BESS rule.
signals.model: ChatGPT Pro extended review + deterministic local verification

signals.title: Crosby County notices Lorenzo BESS tax-abatement consideration
signals.summary: Crosby County gives notice of possible action on a Chapter 312 tax-abatement agreement for Lorenzo Energy Storage’s proposed 70-megawatt, 280-megawatt-hour BESS.
signals.quote:
PURPOSE: The Crosby County Commissioners Court will discuss and take possible action
regarding a tax abatement agreement with Lorenzo Energy Storage, LLC, pursuant to Chapter 312
of the Texas Tax Code.

Property Owner and Applicant (“Applicant”) for Tax Abatement: Lorenzo Energy Storage,
LLC

Proposed Reinvestment Zones: Lorenzo Energy Storage Reinvestment Zone

Property in Proposed Reinvestment Zone: See attached map and description provided in Exhibit
“A” attached hereto and incorporated herein for all purposes.

Anticipated Improvements: The general description of the nature of the improvements included
in the agreement is as follows:

Applicant anticipates constructing 70 Megawatt-AC (280MWh) battery energy storage
system (BESS). Improvements may include (but are not limited to) concreate foundations,
batteries, containers, transformers, inverters, cabling, collection lines, and collection
station.

Code.

signals.title: Carson County reports approval of Data Center Reinvestment Zone #12
signals.summary: Carson County reported that the Commissioners Court had approved Reinvestment Zone #12 for property intended for one or more Fermi data centers and related generation facilities.
signals.quote:
The Carson County Commissioners’ Court will conduct a regularly scheduled public meeting on October
27, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. in the Commissioners Courtroom in Panhandle, Texas, or any regularly scheduled
meeting thereafter, to consider the applications of FermiAmerica, LLC and/or its affiliates (“Fermi”) and
possibly act to approve and execute one or more agreements between the County and Fermi, allowing
for property tax abatements. At a public hearing on August 18, 2025, the Court approved the
designation of certain property located in Carson County as Reinvestment Zone #12 and therefore
eligible for property tax abatement under Chapter 312 of the Texas Tax Code, subject to the Guidelines
and Criteria of the Commissioners Court of Carson County for Granting a Tax Abatement.

Reinvestment Zone #12 is located generally to the southwest of the City of Panhandle, adjacent to the
DOE/NNSA Pantex facility, along and north of Highway 60. Specifically, the property is identified as all or
portions of Sections 2, 3, 4, 5, 9, 10, 11, and 12, Block 2, I&GN RR Co. Survey, Abstract No. 174, and all or
portions of Sections 7, 8, 17, and 18, Block 2, H&GN RR Co. Survey, Abstract No. 156, Carson County,
Texas. The general nature of the improvements consists of the construction, installation, and operation
of one or more data centers as well as the construction, installation, and operation of multiple electrical
energy generation units, and all associated ancillary improvements, facilities, tangible business personal
property, and equipment to support the operations of the data centers and energy generation.
signals.authorityCaveat: This is project-specific facilitation and does not establish a generally applicable local restriction.

signals.title: TX Nazareth Solar amended abatement agreement remains pending
signals.summary: The amended and restated tax-abatement agreement appeared on an agenda and later in a new notice of possible action.
signals.quote:
PURPOSE: The Swisher County Commissioners Court will discuss and take possib le
regarding an Amended and Restated Tax Abatement Agreement with TX Nazareth Solar,
pursuant to Chapter 312 of the Texas Tax Code.

iprgura BAL
le)
a

&
Property Owner and Applicant (“Applicant”) for Tax Abatement: TX Nazareth Solar, "EL
Proposed Reinvestment Zone: TX Nazareth Solar Reinvestment Zone #2

Property in Proposed Reinvestment Zone: See attached map and legal description provided in
Exhibit “A” attached hereto and incorporated herein for all purposes.

Anticipated Improvements: The general description of the nature of the improvements included in
the agreement is as follows:

Applicant anticipates constructing a solar energy facility with up to 200 MW of nameplate
capacity. Improvements may include (but are not limited to) solar modules/panels, metal
mounting system with tracking capabilities, underground conduit, communication cables,
electric collection system wiring, combiner boxes, project substation including breakers, a
transformer, and meters, overhead transmission lines, inverter boxes on concrete pads,
operations and maintenance facility, fencing for safety and security, telephone and internet
communication system, access and service roads, and meteorological equipment.

Summary of Proposed Amendments:

The Amended and Restated Tax Abatement Agreement would reference the new TX Nazareth Solar
Reinvestment Zone #2 as well as extend the Commercial Operations Date so that the Owner agrees
that the Project shall achieve Commercial Operations on or before December 31, 2027, with the option
to extend the Commercial Operations Date to December 31, 2028.

signals.title: Residents urge Austin County to consider a Chapter 391 response to BESS
signals.summary: During public comments, a resident speaking for citizens opposed to BESS asked the Austin County Commissioners Court to consider creating or amending a Chapter 391 agreement; the county judge explained the agreement concept, but the minutes report no county action.
signals.quote:
Bridget Bryan spoke to the court on behalf of citizens against BESS and future
 generations. Would like the court to look at creating a 391 which is to be used on HB150
 to play a larger role on cyber protection. lf the county already has a 391 in place, then it
 is possible that only an amendment would need to take place making an easy fix to it.
 Judge Lapham explained that a 391 is basically an agreement between 2 governing
 bodies and can carry more weight when dealing with a state agency.
signals.authorityCaveat: This is public comment or advocacy; the record does not establish county adoption.

signals.title: Mills County studies solar-abatement and road safeguards
signals.summary: County officials and counsel studied solar reinvestment zones, abatement criteria, agreement procedures, default protections, bonds, and road-use language. No final countywide policy is supplied.
signals.quote:
6. Discussion of Guidelines & Criteria regarding Tax Abatement under Chapter 312 of the
Texas Tax Code. Jeffrey Allen with Kelly Hart Attorneys out of Midland Tx. Joined by zoom
and spoke about getting documents to the court so that they can look over and be familiar with
before the hearing on the matter. The County Attorney had a change he would like to make on
the application and would like to see a more detailed application, Mr. Allen agreed. Judge
Johnson ask about protection if the Solar Farm Co. Defaulted, Mr. Allen said it has been done
before in the Abatement and is not an unreasonable request. Mr. Allen will also include language
for Bond and Road Use.

supportingSources.title: June 16, 2025 12:45 Workshop
supportingSources.quote:
3. Solar Workshop, via Zoom, with attorney Jeffrey Allen, and related matters. — Mr. Allen has
worked with Mills County before with tax abatements on the Bluebonnet/Lantana program. Mr. Allen
addressed the Court with his history of working with landowners and counties on energy-based programs.
Discussed who sets depreciation schedule. The trend for PILOT programs vs no abatements. Solar
projects do not typically create jobs. Allen will confirm the cost per megawatt hour. Possibilities around
the I & S fund to keep the project on the tax rolls. Mr. Allen recommends seeing what the company
proposes. After seeing the proposal, we will have more information about the long-term impact, potential
for jobs, tax rate. No blanket denial. Guidelines for reinvestment zones need to be updated.

signals.title: Hill County adopts agreement for 120 MW Hubbard Wind project
signals.summary: The agreement states that it was adopted at a properly posted Commissioners Court meeting and covers a minimum 120 MW wind facility, including about 40 turbines, within Hill County.
signals.quote:
WHEREAS, this Agreement was adopted at a regularly scheduled meeting of the
Commissioners Court which was preceded by written notice which was properly posted in
accordance with the Open Meetings Act and at which a quorum of the Commissioners Court was
present.

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the recitals set forth above and the mutual
obligations and promises set forth below, and for other good and valuable consideration, the
adequacy and receipt of which are hereby acknowledged, the County and Owner agree as follows:

1. Premises. As used herein “Premises” refers to the real property shown and
described in Exhibit “B” hereto which is attached hereto and incorporated herein. Owner will not

1

own the real property, but rather will be leasing the real property as a lessee. However, Owner will
be the owner of all personal property, fixtures, equipment and related improvements that it installs
on the Premises pursuant to this Agreement. It is likely that not all of the parcels within the
Premises will be leased for the Project.

2. General Project Description. Development and construction/installation in the
Reinvestment Zone of facilities and equipment for, and the operation of a minimum 120-megawatt
AC electric generating wind system/facility, including, but not limited to 40 wind turbines,
transmission lines, operations building, substation, and other equipment. A more specific
identification of the additions to the Premises to be made by Owner is attached as Exhibit “C” hereto
and incorporated by reference herein (hereafter “Project”). Owner is developing the Project as part
of a larger electric generating wind system/facility that includes assets both in the County and in
Limestone County, but this Agreement applies only to the Project in the County.
signals.authorityCaveat: This is project-specific facilitation and does not establish a generally applicable local restriction.
signals.model: ChatGPT Pro extended review + deterministic local verification

signals.title: Prior wind-project depreciation reviewed
signals.summary: A budget workshop compared depreciation experience from earlier wind projects while evaluating incentive terms.
signals.quote:
Budget Workshop — Discussion on Pilot Program Financial Numbers, and related matters
Commissioner Partin talked with Charles Miles to differentiate the difference in abatement
percentages As long as the county 1s making money 1t 1s not an abated tax Charles Miles then
addressed the court with the 265-mullion-dollar evaluation, anticipating the value will decrease
each year, over 10-year period a total tax of $10,279,456 00 Pilot program is asking for a 50%
discount which would roughly be $803,082 50 Mr Miles then addressed the evaluation of the
previous wind projects on the depreciation percentages, due to the numbers the depreciation will
then be more than the 8% number that was given in presentation Commussioner Williams then
asked Mr Miles about variables and methods of collecting income on this Pilot Program project

signals.title: Blue Heron Solar reinvestment zone approved
signals.summary: Mills County approved the reinvestment-zone application for the Blue Heron Solar project.
signals.quote:
6 Consider and Act on Reinvestment Zone, and related matters

Via Zoom with us today !s Bill Pentak of Blue Heron Red River and Jeff Alan, Attorney for Commissioners court

County Judge Jett Johnson considers and acts on proposed solar farms Spoke on if it did pass or not On what percentage
on abatement might be and or not to do a polite program County Judge Jett Johnson stated he 1s not for any abatements

Spoke of list of surrounding several counties that are not giving any abatements Jeff Alan, Attorney, just one point to
make a finding on if you chose to create the investment zone persuade to tax code which already complete public hearing
make binding to solar project would contribute to the retention or the expansion of primary employment or attract major
investing zone Comments from Bil Pentak with Blue Heron Red River to say appreciate and it’s been an honor to the
court for letting me speak in this In terms of the number of counties not giving tax abatements There are 23 counties
that are granted tax abatements or pilots You can go to see a spreadsheet list through the website of Texas Comptroller
who have been granted abatement pilots Where Brown County ts concerned, it did not grant 312 abatements for the
solar farms, but the school district granted 313 abatements at the time, which was far bigger Those have gone away
because of the legislature Even with tax abatement will receive Mills County at least 17 4 million tax revenue, Mullin ISD.
almost 19 million dollars that 1s the revenue they will be able to keep That is, the M&O portion does not have the IN&S
in that portion There are 3 yrs roll back when the ag exempt goes away, the taxes must be paid going back 3 yrs As if
there was no ag exemption That means Mills County will receive in addition to the first year of taxes one-time payment
of $162,000 thousand and not including Road and Bridge will receive $33,000, be about a total of $190 000 thousand
dollars This project will be brought to the County County Judge Jett Johnson mentioned that Red River/ Blue Heron 1s
asking for 50% off on abatement That ts correct just for the equipment, but we will be paying 100% for the land Because
of the ag exemption going away will up to 9,000 to 150, 0000 so the effect abatement will be 34% Discussion or comment
Commissioner Dale Partin asked why the name changed to Bill Pentak The name was the other way around The original
project was named Blue Heron When the project developer is creating a project, it must be named They wanted to call
after something pulled a name out, but they decided to name that came from the area, so there was a flower in the
County Name chiesa County Judge Jett Johnson asks you to have already stated or what the status is Sometime in
summer of 2026 Bill Pentak that construction has not started on the site | know that the Consults have hired top
explosive experts in the country to try to identify the explosion ordinance The construction has not started until the
subject has been obtained Going to vote only in the reinvestment zone

Motion made by accepting the reinvestment zone that was submitted by Blue Heron application for this project
Commissioner Jason Williams and questioned by Commissioner Dale Partin what boundaries just land property Attorney
Jeff Alan answered what ts in the application boundaries Seconded by Commissioner Dale Partin All in favor 3-2 against
County Judge Jett Johnson and Robert Head
signals.authorityCaveat: A reinvestment zone facilitates consideration of a tax abatement and does not itself restrict or finally approve the underlying solar project.

signals.title: Red River solar incentive process authorized
signals.summary: Mills County heard public opposition and road-protection concerns, then voted to begin the Red River reinvestment-zone and tax-abatement process.
signals.quote:
2 Karen Wesson Francis asked the court to vote against any tax incentives or approve the abatements or
any solar energy companies that come into Mills County She suggested that we asked them to enter a
road use agreement or put up a bond to fix roads if they damage them in the process Mrs Francis also
said the jobs, hotels etc will be employed by out-of-county workers not in county like presented Texas
House Bill NO 5 presented in the 88" legislative prohibits school districts from giving tax breaks Also,
Bull NO 819 prohibits taxing authorities from granting property tax abatement to wind and solar
projects Mrs Francis spoke to Knik Chastain Precinct 1 Commussioner out of Brown County, and he
stated they did not give Red Rivier Clean Energy an abatement

4 Consider and Act on approving Accounts Payables

Motion to approve account payables by Commissioner Williams with a second from Commissioner
Sullivan

Motion carried 5-0

5 Consider and Act on Budget Amendments, and related matters
Commissioner Partin questioned where there were two amendments on fuel and oil, Commissioner
Sullivan then commented it must have been a typo 326 Should be tires and 8,000 will be for fuel

1|Page

Motion to approve the budget amendments with corrections by Commussioner Head with a second by _
Commissioner Williams

Motion carried 5-0

6 Consider and Act on Interlocal Agreement with the Bell County Indigent Health Care, and related
matters

Motion to table Interlocal Agreement with Bell County Indigent Health Care until next meeting by
Judge Johnson with a second from Commissioner Partin

Motion carried 5-0
7 Consider and Act on Inmate Healthcare Resolution, and related matters

Mary Gails presented a presentation on source totals report with Mills County Indigent Health Care,
along with a dashboard report showing the largest outpatient care would be inmates at this tme
Currently Indigent Health has processed thirty-three new “clients” There 1s sixty-four clients for the
fiscal year Commussioner Partin questioned the $28,145 28 that was billed from Indigent to Mills
County, the number 1s Medicaid rates Hendricks was outside of the service area and was not willing to
accept Medicare rate until now Mrs Gails stated bills were sent to customers directly instead of
Indigent defense to challenge the rates

Motion to approve the Resolution by Judge Johnson no second
Motion opposed 5-0

Motion to table Inmate Health Care Resolution due to the not having the Resolution in court only the
report by Commissioner Williams with a second by Commissioner Head

Motion carried 5-0

8 Consider and Act on Optional Health Care Services Notification, and related matters

Form presented by Mary Gauls, commussioners reviewed and agreed this was the same form that was
signed last year presented to court Form 3083

Motion to approve Optical Health Care Service Notification by Judge Johnson with a second from
Commissioner Williams

Motion carried 5-0

9 Consider and Act on Election Judges & Polling Locations for the 2025-2027 Election Cycle, and
related matters

Motion to approve Elections Judges & Polling Locations for the 2025-2027 Election Cycle by Judge
Johnson with a second from Commissioner Sullivan

Motion carried 5-0

10 Consider and Act on Double-Wheel Vibrating Roller/Packer Bids for Precinct 1, and related matters
- Commussioner Sullivan presented this information to the court due to the limit being over the
$50,000 00, 1f approved Commissioner Sullivan will post in the paper

2|Page

Motion to approve by Double Wheel Vibrating Roller/Packer Bids for Precinct 1 by Judge Johnson with
a second from Commissioner Sullivan

Motion carried 4-1

11 Consider and Act on whether to begin the Process for Creating a Remvestment Zone at Request of
Red River Clean Energy, and related matters

Motion to table whether to begin the process of creating a remvestment zone by Judge Johnson with no
second

Motion failed for a lack of second

County Attorney Bill Malone stood up and reassured the Commissioners of the language that court
needs to vote on correctly

Motion to table the process of creating a reinvestment zone until the next commissioners court meeting
by Judge Johnson with a second from Commussioner Partin

Motion to table 2-3

Judge Johnson presented to commissioners that he spoke with other counties in the abatement situation
and they all stated they did not approve an abatement Commissioner Partin stated within years the
county will lose money if we move forward with this process by at least eight percent, all commissioners
are concerned'about the fifty percent abatement that was presented by Red River Clean Energy Project
Commussioner Williams would like to talk with Red River Clean Energy Project about a lower
abatement percentage Commissioner Partin, commented about the deficient budget deficit which means
county must make money up somehow, the wind project has caped and amount of money from the
project 1s decreasing

Motion to begin the process of creating investment zone and all details that go along with abatement
process by Commissioner Williams with a second by Commissioner Partin

Motion carned 4-1
signals.authorityCaveat: Beginning an incentive process is project facilitation, not final approval of an abatement or adoption of a solar restriction.

signals.title: Fannin zoning commission recommends against BESS no-permit letter
signals.summary: The Fannin County Zoning Commission approved a recommendation that Commissioners Court not issue GRS BESS Texas Seven LLC a letter stating that no permits were required; fire-code permitting and decommissioning were discussed.
signals.quote:
Item 5. Discussion regarding Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) to be constructed on
Property ID #78404 on Recreation Road 3 in Bonham.

Di Hopkins, Director of Development Services, said that GRS BESS Texas Seven LLC had
requested that the Zoning Commission issue a letter stating that no permitting or permits
were required from the Zoning Commission, and that the company would issue a
decommissioning plan. Chairman Cunningham stated that he was opposed to the
issuance of such a letter. Ms. Hopkins noted that such a letter would have to come from
Commissioners Court and that the issue was on Commissioners Court agenda for August
12.

 1

Troy Hudson, Fannin County Office of Emergency Management and Fannin County Fire
Marshall, said that there would be permits needed under the Fire Code. Chairman
Cunningham noted that the County could not keep battery plants from coming into the
County but did not need to encourage them. Mr. Hudson said that the proposed plant was
in the Bonham Fire District. Mr. Welch noted that part of the property was subject to the
Zoning Commission. Mr. Gilbert asked if this area might be in Bonham’s ETJ, but Ms.
Hopkins said this was not the case.

Steve Cole, a nearby property owner, spoke about his concerns. Rose Schwartz spoke
about her concerns with water quality. Roger Cada spoke about his experience with
battery plants as the Mayor of Savoy and the research he has done. Michael Jeter spoke
about the dangers of lithium.

Mr. Gilbert proposed that the Zoning Commission make a recommendation to
Commissioners Court to not issue a “no permit required” letter. Mr. Darwin seconded the
motion. Mr. Titsworth, Mr. Darwin and Mr. Gilbert voted yes. Chairman Cunningham
abstained. Motion passed.
signals.authorityCaveat: The zoning commission approved a recommendation only; the supplied record does not establish final Commissioners Court action or a countywide permit rule.
signals.model: ChatGPT Pro extended review + deterministic local verification

supportingSources.title: 08/07/2025 Meeting Minutes
supportingSources.quote:
Item 5. Discussion regarding Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) to be constructed on
Property ID #78404 on Recreation Road 3 in Bonham.

Di Hopkins, Director of Development Services, said that GRS BESS Texas Seven LLC had
requested that the Zoning Commission issue a letter stating that no permitting or permits
were required from the Zoning Commission, and that the company would issue a
decommissioning plan. Chairman Cunningham stated that he was opposed to the
issuance of such a letter. Ms. Hopkins noted that such a letter would have to come from
Commissioners Court and that the issue was on Commissioners Court agenda for August
12.

 1

Troy Hudson, Fannin County Office of Emergency Management and Fannin County Fire
Marshall, said that there would be permits needed under the Fire Code. Chairman
Cunningham noted that the County could not keep battery plants from coming into the
County but did not need to encourage them. Mr. Hudson said that the proposed plant was
in the Bonham Fire District. Mr. Welch noted that part of the property was subject to the
Zoning Commission. Mr. Gilbert asked if this area might be in Bonham’s ETJ, but Ms.
Hopkins said this was not the case.

Steve Cole, a nearby property owner, spoke about his concerns. Rose Schwartz spoke
about her concerns with water quality. Roger Cada spoke about his experience with
battery plants as the Mayor of Savoy and the research he has done. Michael Jeter spoke
about the dangers of lithium.

Mr. Gilbert proposed that the Zoning Commission make a recommendation to
Commissioners Court to not issue a “no permit required” letter. Mr. Darwin seconded the
motion. Mr. Titsworth, Mr. Darwin and Mr. Gilbert voted yes. Chairman Cunningham
abstained. Motion passed.

supportingSources.title: Notice of Tax Abatement Consideration - June 23, 2025
supportingSources.quote:
PURPOSE: The Cottle County Commissioners Court will discuss and take possible action regarding

a First Amendment to the Tax Abatement Agreement with Thalia Solar, LLC, pursuant to Chapter 312
of the Texas Tax Code.

Property Owner and Applicant (“Applicant”) for Tax Abatement: Thalia Solar, LLC

Reinvestment Zone: Cottle County Reinvestment Zone - Thalia

Property in Proposed Reinvestment Zone: Sce attached map and property description provided in
Exhibit “A” attached hereto and incorporated herein for all purposes.

Anticipated Improvements: The general description of the nature of the improvements included in
the agreements is as follows:

Applicant anticipates constructing 252 MWAC solar energy generation facility with attached
battery storage. Improvements may include (but are not limited to) Substation, Transmission
Line, Inverters and Transformers, Foundations, Roadways, Paving, & Fencing, Posts &
Racking Equipment, Meteorological Towers & Equipment, Mounting and Tracking
Equipment, Performance Monitoring Equipment Batteries and Containers, Substation, Inverter
and Transformers, Interconnection Facilities, Solar Modules & Panels, Power Conditioning
Equipment, Combiner Boxes, Operation & Maintenance Building, DC and AC Collection
Wires, Cables, and Equipment, SCADA Equipment, Relay Equipment, Foundations, and
Internal Roadways, Paving & Fencing.

Summary of Proposed Amendments:
The First Amendment to the Tax Abatement Agreement would revise the calculations of the
Annual PILOT and extend the Commercial Operations Date so that the Applicant agrees that
the Project shall achieve Commercial Operations on or before December 31, 2027 with the
option to extend the Commercial Operations Date until December 31, 2028.

supportingSources.title: June 23
supportingSources.quote:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING

On Monday, June 23, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., the Cm;nmissioners Court of Colorado County,
Texas at its Regular Meeting shall hold a public hearing in the Colorado County
Courtroom, Colorado County Courthouse, 400 Spring Street, Columbus, Texas 78934 to
consider a request by Cottontail Energy Center, LLC for the designation of land in
Colorado County as a tax abatement reinvestment zone pursuant to Chapter 312, Texas
Tax Code.

At the public hearing, the County will receive information to determine, among other
things, whether (i) the improvements sought within the proposed Zone area are feasible
and practical and would be a benefit to the land to be included in the Zone and the County
after the expiration of any tax abatement agreement; (ii) the proposed Zone designation
would contribute to the retention or expansion of primary employment in the County; and
(iii) the proposed Zone designation would attract major investment in the Zone.

The land being considered for the Zone designation is described in that application for tax
abatement from Cottontail Energy Center, LLC as Colorado County Appraisal District
Parcel ID 62915, being 18.948 acres, more or less, of land located in the W.B. Dewees
Survey, Abstract No. 18, Colorado County, Texas.

At the public hearing, interested persons are invited to speak and present evidence for or
against the Zone designation. Following the public hearing, at the meeting of the Colorado
County Commissioners Court, the County may consider the approval and designation of all
or part of the aforesaid land area as a tax abatement reinvestment zone.

signals.title: Crosby County notices Cone wind-project tax-abatement consideration
signals.summary: Crosby County gives notice of possible action on a Chapter 312 tax-abatement agreement for Cone Renewable Energy Project’s proposed wind generation facility of up to 300 megawatts.
signals.quote:
PURPOSE: The Crosby County Commissioners Court will discuss and take possible action regarding
a tax abatement agreement with Cone Renewable Energy Project, LLC, pursuant to Chapter 312 of the
Texas Tax Code.

Property Owner and Applicant (“Applicant”) for Tax Abatement: Cone Renewable Energy
Project, LLC

Proposed Reinvestment Zones: Cone Renewable Energy Reinvestment Zone

Property in Proposed Reinvestment Zone: See attached map and description provided in Exhibit
“A” attached hereto and incorporated herein for all purposes.

Anticipated Improvements: The general description of the nature of the improvements included in
the agreement is as follows:

Applicant anticipates constructing a wind power electric generation facility with up to 300 MW
of nameplate capacity, of which 150 MW is planned to be built in the County. Improvements
may include (but are not limited to) wind turbines, distribution equipment, advanced control
systems, Operation and Maintenance building, energy distribution equipment, energy
collection/storage systems.
signals.authorityCaveat: The record concerns possible project facilitation and does not establish final approval or a generally applicable local restriction.

signals.title: Fire department checks reported grass fire at solar farm

scene and made contact with foreman of the solar panel farm and they advised
that the fire was out. Fire crew put eyes on where the fire was and determined
that the flre was out. Fire crew cleared the scene and returned to station.

signals.title: Fannin County authorizes regional planning commission for BESS development
signals.summary: Fannin County authorized and approved formation of FIPDC, whose stated purpose includes studying and planning for BESS development within its jurisdiction.
signals.quote:
NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT ORDERED BY THE COMMISSIONERS COURT OF
FANNIN COUNTY, TEXAS, THAT:

Section 1. Authorization. The following actions are hereby authorized:

 (A) The creation and joining of a commission pursuant to Chapter 391, Texas Local
 Government Code to be known as the "Fannin Intergovernmental Planning and
 • Development Commission" (FIPDC) is authorized and approved;

 (B) . Initially, unincorporated area County limits of Fannin County shall be included
 with the City Limits of Savoy to form the boundaries of the newly created FIPDC;
 and

 (C) The Commissioners' Court is authorized to take all steps necessary and required
 under Chapter 391, Texas Local Government Code, to establish and join the
 FIPDC, including cooperating to form appropriate Interlocal Cooperation
 Agreements with Savoy and other municipalities, counties and other governmental
 units affected by the plans of such counties, municipalities and/or governmental
 units as agreed by the voting members of the FIPDC and approved by Fannin
 County and permitted by law.

 Section 2. Purpose. The FIPDC's purpose is to:

 (A) Cooperate and coordinate with local governmental units sharing similar needs to
 plan for matters dealing with health, safety, and general welfare of their
 residents, and the development, including Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS),
 issues affecting the jurisdiction and residents of the FIPDC;

 (B) · Study, research and create a plan to deal with future development of communities,
 areas, and regions regarding BESS facilities located within the jurisdiction of the
 . FIPDC;
signals.authorityCaveat: The action is limited to the identified process or recommendation; broader county effect must not be inferred.

signals.title: ZSS Solar road-use agreement adopted
signals.summary: Knox County approved and adopted a road-use agreement for the ZSS solar project.
signals.quote:
A RESOLUTION AND ORDER APPROVING A ROAD USE AGREEMENT BETWEEN
KNOX COUNTY, TEXAS AND ZSS POWER, LLC

WHEREAS, Knox County, Texas (the “County” or Knox County) is familiar with the solar energy
project contemplated by ZSS Power, LLC (28S Solar) in the portion of the county described on Exhibit A,
attached hereto and incorporated by reference.

WHEREAS, ZSS Solar contemplates making certain improvements to the real property located
within the Project Area consisting of solar powered electrical power generating facility (“Improvements”);

WHEREAS, no part of the Project Area is located within the city limits of any towns within Knox
County, and no part of the Project Area is located within the extraterritorial jurisdictions of any other city
within the County.

WHEREAS, the ownership, construction, operations and maintenance of the Improvements will
require access to, egress from, encroachments into, crossing of, and possibly upgrades to (“Road Usage”)
County roads, County owned rights-of-way, and County-held right-of-way easements located in Knox
County, Texas;

WHEREAS, by its dedication, use, and/or maintenance of the roads and as the owner and holder
of County rights-of-way and right-of-way easements in the County, the Commissioners Court of Knox
County, Texas have authority on behalf of the County to permit such Road Usage;

WHEREAS, ZSS Solar seeks the County’s permission for such Road Usage and Knox County, Texas
has agreed to grant said permission.

NOW, THEREFORE,
BE IT ORDERED BY THE COMMISSIONERS COURT OF KNOX COUNTY, TEXAS:

4. That the findings and recitals in the preamble to this Order are found to be true and correct
and are hereby RATIFIED, APPROVED and ADOPTED.
signals.authorityCaveat: This is project-specific road coordination, not a countywide solar restriction.
signals.model: ChatGPT Pro extended review + deterministic local verification

supportingSources.title: Nov 2025 Rolling Plains Abatement Agreement-first 12 page
supportingSources.quote:
Tax Abatement Agreement
between
Knox County, Texas, and Rolling Plains Solar, LLC

State of Texas
County of Knox

This Tax Abatement Agreement (this “Agreement”) is made and entered into by and between
Knox County, Texas (the “County”), acting through its duly elected officers, and Rolling
Plains Solar, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, to be located on a portion of the tract
of land within the Rolling Plains Solar Reinvestment Zone, more specifically described in
Attachment A to this Agreement. This Agreement becomes effective upon final signature by
both parties (the “Effective Date”) and remains in effect until fulfillment of the obligations

described in Section IV herein (the “Term”), unless terminated earlier as provided herein.
Recitals

WHEREAS, the County indicated its election to be eligible to participate in tax abatements
and established the Knox County Tax Abatement Guidelines and Criteria (the “Guidelines”)
by resolution most recently updated on or about November 18, 2024;

WHEREAS, the Commissioners Court of Knox County, Texas (the “County Commissioners
Court”) desires to promote economic development within its jurisdiction as authorized by the
Property Redevelopment and Tax Abatement Act, as amended (Texas Tax Code § 312.001, et
seq.), and the Guidelines;

WHEREAS, on November 10, 2025, a hearing before the County Commissioners Court was
held, such date being at least seven (7) days after the date of publication of the notice of such
public hearing in the local newspaper of general circulation in the County and the delivery of
written notice to the respective presiding officers of each taxing entity that includes within its
boundaries real property that is to be included in the Reinvestment Zone (as defined below);

WHEREAS, the County Commissioners Court, after conducting a hearing, having heard
evidence and testimony, and prior to considering this Agreement, found, based on theevidence
and testimony presented to it, the Reinvestment Zone met the criteria set forth in Chapter 312
of the Texas Tax Code for the creation of a reinvestment zone as set forth in the Property
Redevelopment and Tax Abatement Act, as amended, and the Guidelines, in that it was
reasonably likely as a result of the designation to contribute to the retention or expansion of
primary employment or to attract investment in the zone that would be a benefit to the property
and contribute to the economic development of the County, and that the entire tract of land was
located entirely within an unincorporated area of the County;

WHEREAS, entering into this Agreement will serve the best interests of the County and its
citizens and comply with the Guidelines by:

A. enhancing and diversifying the economic and industrial bases of the County;

Knox County ~ Rolling Plains Solar, LLC- Tax Abatement Agreement Page !

B. contributing to the retention and expansion of primary employment; and

C. attracting major investment that will be of benefit to and contribute to the economic
development of the County;

WHEREAS, the contemplated use of the Site (as defined below) and the contemplated
Improvements (as defined below) as set forth in this Agreement, and the other terms of this
Agreement will encourage development of the Reinvestment Zone, are in accordance with the
purposes for its creation, and are in compliance with the Guidelines and all applicable laws;

WHEREAS, Owner's (as defined below) use of the Site is expected to favorably influence the
economic and employment base of the County;

WHEREAS, the County finds that the Improvements sought are feasible and practical and will
be of benefit to the real property located in the Reinvestment Zone, to the Site, and to the
County after expiration of this Agreement;

WHEREAS, the County finds that the terms of this Agreement and the proposed Improvements
and Eligible Property subject to this Agreement meet the Guidelines;

WHEREAS, acopy of this Agreement has been furnished, in the manner prescribed by law, to
the presiding officers of the governing bodies of each of the taxing units in which the property
subject to this Agreement is located; and

WHEREAS, this Agreement was approved. at a regularly scheduled meeting of the County
Commissioners Court in accordance with the Open Meetings Act, as amended, preceded by at
least thirty (30) days by publication of notice of such public hearing in compliance withSection
312.207 of the Texas Tax Code; and

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of these Recitals, premises, the promises, mutual
covenants, and agreements contained in this Agreement, and for other good and valuable
consideration, the receipt and adequacy of which are hereby acknowledged, the County and
Owner agree as follows:

supportingSources.title: Final Rolling Plains Tax Abatement
supportingSources.quote:
Tax Abatement Agreement
between
Knox County, Texas, and Rolling Plains Solar, LLC

State of Texas
County of Knox

This Tax Abatement Agreement (this “Agreement”) is made and entered into by and between
Knox County, Texas (the “County”), acting through its duly elected officers, and Rolling
Plains Solar, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, to be located on a portion of the tract
of land within the Rolling Plains Solar Reinvestment Zone, more specifically described in
Attachment A to this Agreement. This Agreement becomes effective upon final signature by
both parties (the “Effective Date”) and remains in effect until fulfillment of the obligations
described in Section IV herein (the “Term”), unless terminated earlier as provided herein.

Recitals

WHEREAS, the County indicated its election to be eligible to participate in tax abatements
and established the Knox County Tax Abatement Guidelines and Criteria (the “Guidelines”)
by resolution most recently updated on or about November 18, 2024;

WHEREAS, the Commissioners Court of Knox County, Texas (the “County Commissioners
Court”) desires to promote economic development within its jurisdiction as authorized by the
Property Redevelopment and Tax Abatement Act, as amended (Texas Tax Code § 312.001, et
seq.), and the Guidelines;

WHEREAS, on November 10, 2025, a hearing before the County Commissioners Court was
held, such date being at least seven (7) days after the date of publication of the notice of such
public hearing in the local newspaper of general circulation in the County and the delivery of
written notice to the respective presiding officers of each taxing entity that includes within its
boundaries real property that is to be included in the Reinvestment Zone (as defined below);

WHEREAS, the County Commissioners Court, after conducting a hearing, having heard
evidence and testimony, and prior to considering this Agreement, found, based on the evidence
and testimony presented to it, the Reinvestment Zone met the criteria set forth in Chapter 312
of the Texas Tax Code for the creation of a reinvestment zone as set forth in the Property
Redevelopment and Tax Abatement Act, as amended, and the Guidelines, in that it was
reasonably likely as a result of the designation to contribute to the retention or expansion of
primary employment or to attract investment in the zone that would be a benefit to the property
and contribute to the economic development of the County, and that the entire tract of land was
located entirely within an unincorporated area of the County;

WHEREAS, entering into this Agreement will serve the best interests of the County and its
citizens and comply with the Guidelines by:

A. enhancing and diversifying the economic and industrial bases of the County;

Knox County - Rolling Plains Solar, LLC- Tax Abatement Agreement Page |

Docusign Envelope ID: CFACC6F9-C558-4A4F-ACDC-FA0C7E304F26

B. contributing to the retention and expansion of primary employment; and

C. attracting major investment that will be of benefit to and contribute to the economic
development of the County;

WHEREAS, the contemplated use of the Site (as defined below) and the contemplated
Improvements (as defined below) as set forth in this Agreement, and the other terms of this
Agreement will encourage development of the Reinvestment Zone, are in accordance with the
purposes for its creation, and are in compliance with the Guidelines and all applicable laws;

WHEREAS, Owner’s (as defined below) use of the Site is expected to favorably influence the
economic and employment base of the County;

WHEREAS, the County finds that the Improvements sought are feasible and practical and will
be of benefit to the real property located in the Reinvestment Zone, to the Site, and to the
County after expiration of this Agreement;

WHEREAS, the County finds that the terms of this Agreement and the proposed Improvements
and Eligible Property subject to this Agreement meet the Guidelines;

WHEREAS, a copy of this Agreement has been furnished, in the manner prescribed by law, to
the presiding officers of the governing bodies of each of the taxing units in which the property
subject to this Agreement is located; and

WHEREAS, this Agreement was approved at a regularly scheduled meeting of the County
Commissioners Court in accordance with the Open Meetings Act, as amended, preceded by at
least thirty (30) days by publication of notice of such public hearing in compliance with Section
312.207 of the Texas Tax Code; and

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of these Recitals, premises, the promises, mutual
covenants, and agreements contained in this Agreement, and for other good and valuable

consideration, the receipt and adequacy of which are hereby acknowledged, the County and
Owner agree as follows:

signals.quote:
RESOLUTION OF THE COMMISSIONERS COURT
OF KNOX COUNTY, TEXAS
DESIGNATING THE ROLLING PLAINS SOLAR REINVESTMENT ZONE

A RESOLUTION DESIGNATING A CERTAIN AREA AS A REINVESTMENT ZONE
FOR A COMMERCIAL/INDUSTRIAL TAX ABATEMENT IN KNOX COUNTY,
TEXAS, ESTABLISHING THE BOUNDARIES THEREOF, AND PROVIDING FOR AN
EFFECTIVE DATE.

WHEREAS, the Commissioners Court of Knox County, Texas desires to promote the
development or redevelopment of a certain contiguous geographic area within its jurisdiction by
the creation of a reinvestment zone as authorized by the Property Redevelopment and Tax
Abatement Act, as amended (Texas Property Tax Code § 312.001, ef seg.); and

WHEREAS, the Commissioners Court of Knox County, Texas has previously adopted
Guidelines and Criteria of the Commissioners Court of Knox County for Granting a Tax
Abatement in Reinvestment Zone Created in Knox County, Texas (the “Guidelines”); and

WHEREAS, on this date, a hearing before the Commissioners Court of Knox County,
Texas was held, such date being at least seven (7) days after the date of publication of the notice
of such public hearing in the local newspaper of general circulation in Knox County and the
delivery of written notice to the respective presiding officers of each taxing entity that includes
within its boundaries real property that is to be included in the proposed reinvestment zone; and

WHEREAS, the Commissioners Court of Knox County, Texas at such public hearing
invited any interested person to appear and speak for or against the creation of the reinvestment

zone and whether all or part of the territory described should be included in the proposed
reinvestment zone; and

WHEREAS, the proponents of the reinvestment zone offered evidence, both oral and.
documentary, in favor of all of the foregoing matters relating to the creation of the reinvestment

zone and opponents, if any, of the reinvestment zone appeared to contest the creation of the
reinvestment zone.

BE IT RESOLVED BY THE COMMISSIONERS COURT OF KNOX COUNTY, TEXAS:

SECTION 1. That the facts and recitations contained in the preamble of this Resolution
are hereby found and declared to be true and correct.

SECTION 2. That the Commissioners Court of Knox County, Texas, after conducting
such hearing and having heard such evidence and testimony, has made the following findings
and determinations based on the evidence and testimony presented to it:

(a) That the public hearing on adoption of the Rolling Plains Solar Reinvestment
Zone has been properly called, held and conducted and that notice of such hearing

supportingSources.title: Rolling Plains Solar Reinvestment Zone
supportingSources.quote:
SECTION 3. That pursuant to the Property Redevelopment and Tax Abatement Act, as
amended, and the Guidelines, Knox County Commissioner’s Court hereby creates the Rolling
Plains Solar Reinvestment Zone; a reinvestment zone for commercial-industrial tax abatement
encompassing only the area described on and as shown on the map in “Exhibit A”, and such
reinvestment zone is hereby designated and shall hereafter be referred to as Rolling Plains Solar
Reinvestment Zone.

SECTION 4. That Rolling Plains Solar Reinvestment Zone shall take effect on the date
of this Resolution and shall remain designated as a commercial-industrial reinvestment zone for
a period of five (5) years from such date of designation and may be renewed for an additional
five (5) year period thereafter.

SECTION 5. That if any section, paragraph, clause or provision of this Resolution shall
for any reason beheld to be invalid or unenforceable, the invalidity or unenforceability of such
section, paragraph, clause, or provision shall not affect any of the remaining provisions of this
Resolution.

SECTION 6. That it is hereby found, determined and declared that a sufficient notice of
the date, hour, place and subject, of the meeting of the Knox County Commissioners Court at
which this Resolution was adopted was posted at a place convenient and readily accessible at all
times as required by the Texas Open Government Act, Texas Government Code, Chapter 551, as
amended, and that a public hearing was held prior to the designation of such reinvestment zone
and that proper notice of the hearing was published in the official newspaper of general
circulation within the County, and furthermore, such notice was in fact delivered to the presiding
officer of any affected taxing entity as prescribed by the Property Redevelopment and Tax
Abatement Act.

Knox County Resolution — Rolling Plains Solar Reinvestment Zone Page2

PASSED, APPROVED AND ADOPTED on this the 10" day of November 2025

of the Texas Tax Code for the creation of a reinvestment zone as set forth in the Property
Redevelopment and Tax Abatement Act, as amended, and the Guidelines, in that it was
reasonably likely as a result of the designation to contribute to the retention or expansion of
primary employment or to attract investment in the zone that would be a benefit to the property
and contribute to the economic development of the County, and that the entire tract of land was
located entirely within an unincorporated area of the County;

WHEREAS, entering into this Agreement will serve the best interests of the County and its
citizens and comply with the Guidelines by:

A. enhancing and diversifying the economic and industrial bases of the County;

Knox County — Wagon Timber Solar, LLC Tax Abatement Agreement Page|

B. contributing to the retention and expansion of primary employment; and

C. attracting major investment that will be of benefit to and contribute to the economic
development of the County;

WHEREAS, the contemplated use of the Site (as defined below) and the contemplated
Improvements (as defined below) as set forth in this Agreement, and the other terms of this
Agreement are expected to encourage development of the Reinvestment Zone, are in
accordance with the purposes for its creation, and are in compliance with the Guidelines and
all applicable laws;

WHEREAS, Owner’s (as defined below) use of the Site is expected to favorably influence the
economic and employment base of the County;

WHEREAS, the County finds that the Improvements sought are feasible and practical and will
be of benefit to the real property located in the Reinvestment Zone, to the Site, and to the
County after expiration of this Agreement;

WHEREAS, the County finds that the terms of this Agreement and the proposed Improvements
and Eligible Property subject to this Agreement meet the Guidelines;

WHEREAS, a copy of this Agreement has been furnished, in the manner prescribed by law, to
the presiding officers of the governing bodies of each of the taxing units in which the property
subject to this Agreement is located; and

WHEREAS, this Agreement was approved at a regularly scheduled meeting of the County
Commissioners Court in accordance with the Open Meetings Act, as amended, preceded by at
least thirty (30) days by publication of notice of such public hearing in compliance with Section
312.207 of the Texas Tax Code; and

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of these Recitals, premises, the promises, mutual
covenants, and agreements contained in this Agreement, and for other good and valuable
consideration, the receipt and adequacy of which are hereby acknowledged, the County and
Owner agree as follows:

supportingSources.title: Wagon Timber Solar Abatement Agreement part 2
supportingSources.quote:
This Agreement contains the entire and integrated Tax Abatement Agreement between the
County and Owner, and supersedes any and all other negotiations and agreements, whether
written or oral, between the parties. This Agreement has not been executed in reliance upon
any representation or promise except those contained herein.

XVI. Coordination of Local Hiring and Services

A. Owner shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maximize its use of County labor
and services and supplies purchased from County businesses in the course of
performing under this Agreement, as is further described in the Local Spending and
Support Plan attached to this Agreement as Attachment B.

B. For every year during the Term after the COD, Owner, its contractors, and their
respective affiliates will collectively employ at least one (1) full-time Project employee
in the County.

C. Upon request by Owner, County shall provide a written statement certifying that Owner
is then in compliance (or has fully complied) with the Local Spending and Support
Plan; if County cannot make such statement, County will provide an explanation to
Owner of its determination.

XVII. Road and Bridge Maintenance

During construction of the Improvements, Owner agrees to use commercially reasonable
efforts to minimize the disruption to County roads (for purposes of this paragraph, the term
“roads” includes, without limitation, all adjacent ditches and rights-of-way), culverts, and
bridges and agrees to repair any damage caused to County roads, culverts, or bridges by Owner
or its agents. After construction, Owner will leave such County roads, culverts, and bridges in
a state of equal or better condition than they were in prior to construction, excepting normal
wear and tear. Any upgrade or requirement to upgrade any road, culvert, or bridge used or
necessary for Owner’s operations will be borne solely by Owner. After construction, the
County will only be responsible for the normal routine maintenance of the County roads,
culverts, and bridges, and Owner will be responsible for any extraordinary repair or
maintenance of County roads, culverts, and/or bridges that becomes necessary or appropriate
due to the use of such roads, culverts, and bridges by Owner or its agents. All repairs,
maintenance, replacements, and upgrades will be made in accordance with County standards
and specifications, and Owner will only use such materials in repairing, maintaining, replacing,
and upgrading County roads, culverts, and bridges as are acceptable to the County, in the
County’s sole discretion. In the event of any breach of this Section XVII, County’s exclusive
remedy shall be to receive reimbursement from Owner for the reasonable cost of any repairs
County may cause to be constructed following Owner’s failure to do so in a timely manner
following notice of such failure from County. This Section XVII shall become void and shall
have no further effect upon the execution of a separate Agreement for Road Use, Repair, and
Improvement by and between Owner and County relating to the Project.

Knox County — Wagon Timber Solar, LLC- Tax Abatement Agreement Page 16

XVIII. Site Maintenance

Owner shall maintain the Site reasonably free from accumulation of objectionable, unsightly,
or unsanitary matter, debris, waste material, rubbish, tumbleweeds, and noxious weeds.

XIX. Indemnity

Owner agrees to indemnify, defend, and hold the County, each of its elected officials, all of its
servants, agents, and employees, any person or legal entity designated by the County to
perform any function required under the Guidelines, under the tax abatement application, or
by the terms of this Agreement, and the Appraisal District, its officers, directors, servants,
agents and employees (collectively, the “Indemnitees”) harmless from any and all claims,
demands, liabilities, losses, costs, actions, causes of action, and attorneys’ fees incurred by or
alleged against the Indemnitees (“Claims”) arising from or in any way relating to the tax
abatement application, the terms, covenants, and conditions contained in this Agreement, and
the actions contemplated by this Agreement; provided, Owner shall not be responsible to
indemnify the Indemnitees for Claims arising from the gross negligence or intentional
misconduct of the Indemnitees. This provision does not waive any governmental immunity
available to the Indemnitees under Texas law, nor does this provision waive any defense of
any party under Texas law.

XX. Reimbursement of Expenses

Within thirty (30) days of the date of receipt of an invoice, Owner agrees to reimburse the
County for or pay directly to the County’s attorneys, as applicable, the reasonable and
necessary attorney’s fees and expenses incurred, directly or indirectly, by the County in
connection with the negotiation and formalization of the Abatement and this Agreement in an
amount not to exceed Seventeen Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($17,500.00).

XXI. Estoppel Certificates.

Each party on written request from the other party shall provide an estoppel certificate that
shall certify, as of the date of the certificate: (i) that this Agreement is in full force and effect
without default if such is the case, (ii) the remaining term of this Agreement, and (iii) such
other matters as may be agreed upon by the parties, a party’s consent to inclusion of other
matters not to be unreasonably withheld. A party shall provide the estoppel certificate or an
explanation of why the party is not willing to provide the certificate within thirty (30) days of
receiving a request.

XXII. Employment of Undocumented Workers.

During the term of this Agreement, Owner agrees not to knowingly employ any undocumented
workers as defined in Section 2264.001 of the Texas Government Code. If Owner is convicted
after exhaustion of all rights of appeal of a violation under 8 U.S.C. §1324a(f), Owner shall
repay the amount of the abatements and any other funds actually then received by Owner from
the County as of the date of such violation, not later than one hundred and twenty (120) days
after the date Owner is notified by the County of a violation of this section, plus interest at the
rate periodically announced by the Wall Street Journal as the prime or base commercial lending

Knox County — Wagon Timber Solar, LLC- Tax Abatement Agreement Page 17

rate, or if the Wall Street Journal shall ever cease to exist or cease to announce a prime or base
lending rate, then at the annual rate of interest from time to time announced by Citibank, N.A.
(or by any other New York money center bank selected by the County) as its prime or base
commercial lending rate. The payment of interest shall be as if it had been accruing from the
dates the abatements were granted to Owner until the date the amount due is repaid to the
County.

XXIII. No Boycott.

In accordance with Chapter 2270 of the Texas Government Code, a Texas governmental entity
may not enter into an agreement with a business entity for the provision of goods or services
unless the agreement contains a written verification from the business entity that it: (1) does
not boycott Israel; and (2) will not boycott Israel during the term of the agreement. Chapter
2270 of the Texas Government Code does not apply to (1) a company that is a sole
proprietorship; (2) a company that has fewer than ten (10) full-time employees; or (3) the
contract has a value of less than One Hundred Thousand Dollars ($100,000.00). Unless Owner
is not subject to Chapter 2270 of the Texas Government Code for any of the reasons stated
herein, the signatory executing this Agreement on behalf of Owner verifies that Owner verifies
that Owner does not boycott Israel and will not boycott Israel during the term of this
Agreement.

XXIV. Nota Listed Company.
In accordance with Section 2252.152 of the Texas Government Code, the Owner covenants
and agrees that Owner is not on a list maintained by the State Comptroller’s office prepared
and maintained pursuant to Section 2252.153 of the Texas Government Code.
XXV. No Firearms Boycott.
To the extent Texas Government Code Chapter 2274 applies to this Agreement, Owner
represents that: (i) Owner does not have a practice, policy, guidance, or directive that
discriminates against a firearm entity or firearm trade association; and (ii) Owner will not
discriminate during the term against a firearm entity or firearm trade association.
XXVI. No Energy Company Boycott.

To the extent Texas Government Code Chapter 2276 applies to this Agreement, Owner
represents that: (i) Owner does not boycott energy companies; and (ii) Owner will not boycott

energy companies during the term.

(remainder of page intentionally left blank)

Knox County — Wagon Timber Solar, LLC- Tax Abatement Agreement Page 18

signals.title: Wagon Timber BESS included in approved tax abatement
signals.summary: The approved Wagon Timber agreement includes the project's battery energy storage facility among the abated improvements.
signals.quote:
WHEREAS, this Agreement was approved at a regularly scheduled meeting of the County
Commissioners Court in accordance with the Open Meetings Act, as amended, preceded by at
least thirty (30) days by publication of notice of such public hearing in compliance with Section
312.207 of the Texas Tax Code; and

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of these Recitals, premises, the promises, mutual
covenants, and agreements contained in this Agreement, and for other good and valuable
consideration, the receipt and adequacy of which are hereby acknowledged, the County and
Owner agree as follows:

I. Authorization

This Agreement is authorized and governed by Chapter 312 of the Texas Tax Code, as
amended, and by the Guidelines.

IL. Definitions
As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meaning set forth below:
A. “Abatement” means the full or partial exemption from ad valorem taxes on property

in a Reinvestment Zone as provided herein and in no event can the duration of the
Abatement period exceed ten (10) years as described in Section IV(B).

Knox County — Wagon Timber Solar, LLC— Tax Abatement Agreement Page 2

B. “Abatement Period” means the ten-year period designated in Section IV(B).
C. “Base Year” means the Calendar Year in which the Effective Date occurs.

D. “Battery Facility” means Eligible Property meeting the definition for “improvements”
or “tangible personal property” provided by Chapter 1 of the Texas Tax Code and
includes, without limitation, a Battery Energy Storage System (BESS), concrete
foundations, inverters and transformers, foundations, containers, cabling, collection
lines, and collection station, energy storage cooling systems, fire suppression systems,
and interconnection facilities, for the purpose of storing electricity generated, in whole
or in part, on the Site.
signals.authorityCaveat: The record concerns incentive coverage within a project agreement, not a BESS restriction.
